purpose: Provides ranked leading indicators with specific lead times and hit rates for timing stock-bond correlation regime flips from positive/unstable to negative, ordered by the economic transmission sequence.
components: Tier 1: IG credit spreads widening >75bp from trough — 18-month lead, 6/7 historical hit rate
Tier 2a: ISM manufacturing <47 for 2+ consecutive months — 12-month lead, 5/6 hit rate
Tier 2b: Yield curve un-inversion — 6-12 month lead, 4/5 hit rate
Tier 3: Initial claims >300K — 3-6 month lead, 7/8 hit rate
Tier 4: Fed cumulative cuts >100bp — 1-3 month lead, ~100% hit rate
Sequencing logic: credit markets (institutional, fastest) → manufacturing (real economy) → labor market (lagged) → Fed response (institutional)
current_assessment: As of March 2026, only Tier 2b (yield curve un-inversion) is active. Probability of correlation flip within 12 months is below the historical base rate for multi-tier confirmation. Next critical signals to monitor: IG OAS crossing 150bp and ISM sustaining below 47.
limitations: Based on only 7 transitions — small sample limits statistical power
Hit rates are approximate, not precise probabilities
May not account for structural changes: 50% passive AUM, $800B+ basis trade, fiscal dominance
Fiscal stalemate transitions may follow different signpost patterns than clean monetary transitions
Does not distinguish between demand-shock and supply-shock driven flips
